question_text: Which surface would create the most friction with a sliding block: sandpaper, ice, or polished wood?
explanation: Sandpaper has a rough and uneven surface with many microscopic bumps and crevices. These irregularities increase friction because they create more resistance when a block slides across it. In contrast, ice and polished wood are smooth surfaces, so they produce less friction. Smoother surfaces mean fewer obstructions to the sliding block's movement.
